But yeah, essentially we would bill it per property if that's how you prefer.

Ok.

I don't imagine that each property will have will need this type of information.

Um, I think this info or this, uh, technology would be pretty vital for that.

It's an older property though, not a market rate.
Um kind of like smaller, I think it's like maybe 22 units.
Um So it's nothing crazy.
But again, they kind of wanted to, there's a problem with their pricing and rent over there basically.

So, is that something that if we were like, hey, Austin, we wanna add, you know, five properties in the next month or next two months.
Is that something?
How would that process work?

Yeah, so you can add, um just because you can add properties at any time just because the way that we're able to like as you saw with the trial, basically, you just would tell us the, the property name and address and we could set that up, you know, within, I mean, it'd be set up within the week.
Um, so, uh, you can always add that on to your contract.
Um, and then we would be able to just near that with whatever your initial um, contract term is.
So your, your standard contract is just the annual one year contract.

Um And then if it was like in the middle of the year, you know, and you had that situation where you want to add others on, we could add them on from whenever that time is and then it would end at that initial contract, end date then too.
So it matches up.

So, so if we were, um, you know, six months into the year and we chose to add on five more than those five more that are added at the halfway point of the year would only go for that six months until the term is up.
Exactly.
Just so that everything matches nicely for you and it's, you know, under one umbrella, if you will, makes sense.
Makes sense.
Ok.
Well, um, you know, that, that seems like that is, uh, you know, the bulk of what I wanted to know, I mean, I was fine with whatever I saw when I looked at it with Chad.

Um, so I think we're pretty, you know, at least I'm good in terms of what we're able to do and it seemed like it was pretty valuable, uh, if you can refresh, like what the information is that they give you.
I remember there was, um, the pricing, the incentives that they're doing and what else is there that we can find out?
Yeah, I can quickly pop through it.
Um, I have it up right here if you can see.

So you're gonna get, um you know, your total units, units available.
Uh The rents obviously, and then we're what really makes us unique.

Um And then we'll also show you, um you can quickly get averages then by the floor plans.
So like under this unit type tab, you know, hey, what's the two bed going for right now in the Cathedral market?
You could easily see that's going for 2437 um in the last seven days, it's trending up basically a full percent right now.
Um And then as well as you get the trailing 12 weeks, so you can see all the peaks and valleys.
So right back here, um it's stated, you know, on the sixth of November, you can see that it was at 2477 right?
Um So really, um, I guess I would just ask you what metrics are most important?
Are you looking for it generally?
Um Well, really, the, the the cost of the rent obviously will be like the first one.
And then just all of this information honestly is pertinent.

Um to be honest, like in terms of the unit type, the size of the unit, the um the concessions, everything that we see here is very pertinent because this is stuff that we can use to again compete in the market.
So, um I don't think there's a hierarchy of what's important.
I just kind of wanted to know an overall of what for sure.
I just didn't know sometimes I, if I could just point out because generally, I mean, we pretty much have everything.
The only thing that you're not gonna be able to have that sometimes uh partners ask for is the traffic.
Um Just because that's a really, um and the reason behind that is it's pretty subjective number, everybody really kind of tracks traffic differently.
Uh Like, for example, sometimes people have told us that they count traffic, like even if a current resident walks into the leasing office.
So, uh you know, that's really, it's really kind of a, a number that isn't very consistent anyway.
Um So that's really the other than traffic, you're gonna be able to get everything on here.
So yeah, we're gonna organize by floor plan, the square footage um concessions as you saw too.
If they have concessions listed, we we'll be able to collect that and uh calculate net effective rent for you uh days on market too.
I know is a metric a lot of folks like.
So if you scroll all the way over on the right hand side, you can see average days on market so you can filter that high to low and see who's moving the fastest and who isn't.
Um And once again, we'll organized by floor plan if you have any questions too, on specific properties, this units type tab.
Um We'll really show you every single unit that's available uh in the com set.
So you can really filter that down if you want to just look at specific properties, you can filter by that as well.
Um So it's really easy to just filter stuff down to whatever you're looking for.
Nice.
OK.
Awesome.
